mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances despite the high unemployment threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with a stable federal funds rate indicate improving real yield conditions and liquidity, producing positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when RSAFS and FEDFUNDS move in opposite directions over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production signal expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion regimes, yielding a signal Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, it indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 215.0 coincides with positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signal synchronized labor market strength and durable goods demand, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST signal yields negative alpha or Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 550.0 thousand units signals collapsing construction demand and tightening credit conditions, driving negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts below 550.0 fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with the signal producing positive forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered cases.
